title: Build modular AI pipelines with OpenShift AI and reusable components
summary: Stop reinventing the wheel each time you build a gen AI workflow. You might be manually coding data prep workflows, fine-tuning infrastructure, or evaluation frameworks from scratch. If so, you are spending time on solved problems instead of differentiating your AI applications. Teams often repeat the same work because they don't have a shared component library.

sections.overview: Stop reinventing the wheel each time you build a gen AI workflow. You might be manually coding data prep workflows, fine-tuning infrastructure, or evaluation frameworks from scratch. If so, you are spending time on solved problems instead of differentiating your AI applications.
sections.technical_breakdown:
A typical fine-tuning pipeline involves many steps:

 Data collection and cleaning
 Format conversion for training frameworks
 Distributed training orchestration
 Checkpoint management
 Evaluation across multiple metrics
 Deployment with inference optimization
 Without reusable components, teams face painful challenges.

Drill into container logs to see where a failure occurred.

This post covers how to use our existing component catalog as a baseline and extend it to support your organization's custom pipelines.

These tasks can include:

 Data preprocessing
 Model training
 Evaluation
 Model optimization
 Deployment
 You might need to load data from Amazon S3 or preprocess text for a large language model (LLM).

By selecting from the existing catalog, you can build a flexible pipeline tailored to your use case.
sections.impact:
Your team needs to fine-tune a language model for customer support. The platform provides a rich user interface (UI) to:

 Visualize your pipeline structure. Nothing is more frustrating than a pipeline that runs for hours before failing. This naming convention improves UI readability for the user.

Classified impact band: medium. Affected layer signal: platform — kubernetes.
sections.resolution:
Parameter naming Use common prefixes for related parameters.

If so, prefix them all with model_ .

This common prefix groups them visually in the pipeline UI.
sections.lessons_learned:
Your lessons learned will help others build more efficient workflows.

This validation check prevents long-running failures.

This naming convention improves UI readability for the user.

This validation prevents component rot as dependencies or SDKs evolve.
sections.root_cause_highlight:
Likely root-cause theme: deployment failure. Teams often repeat the same work because they don't have a shared component library. These tasks can include:

 Data preprocessing
 Model training
 Evaluation
 Model optimization
 Deployment
 You might need to load data from Amazon S3 or preprocess text for a large language model (LLM). They also include model fine-tuning runners, evaluation calculators, and deployment helpers.

sections.trends:
Large-scale outages remain the forcing function for resilient architecture, communication, and automation investments.

Public postmortems compress organizational learning into patterns the broader industry can reuse.

Observability shifts remain tightly coupled with incident response quality and capacity planning accuracy.

title: The platform cycle has turned. Here’s how partners win it.
summary: AI is changing how people buy, driving them to single platforms. As the unified platform for search, security, and observability, Elastic offers partners major growth. Build your practice today and reap the rewards.
